seoTitle: Port Botany vs Port Hacking — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Botany and Port Hacking. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Botany vs Port Hacking: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Botany and Port Hacking.
heading: Port Botany vs Port Hacking — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Botany, New South Wales and Port Hacking, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Botany has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Port Hacking (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Botany or Port Hacking safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Botany has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Port Hacking scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Botany scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Port Hacking scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Botany or Port Hacking?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Botany has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
